title: Stock Market Today: Dow Rises, Nasdaq & S&P 500 Fall; Oil Slides; Chips Tumble
summary: Stock Market Today: Dow Rises, Nasdaq & S&P 500 Fall; Oil Slides; Chips Tumble
extracted_text: DJIA 51,848.90 (+0.35%), S&P 500 7,358.22 (-0.10%), Nasdaq 25,476.64 (-0.43%), Russell 2000 2,986.63 (+0.37%). U.S. 10 Yr 4.391% (-2.47%). VIX 18.63 (-4.41%). WTI Crude Oil $69.81 (-0.75%), settled down 3.9% at $70.34. Brent $73.36 (-0.69%). Bitcoin $61,022.01 (+1.91%). Gold $4,016.40 (-3.21%). DXY 101.58 (+0.17%). SOXX -2.6%. MAGS -0.7%. Equal-weight S&P 500 +0.8%. Margin debt surged to $1.4 trillion in May, +54% YoY — all-time high for leverage. Mark Hackett (Nationwide): 'Investor leverage has reached historic levels... reflecting the lottery mentality of some retail investors.' Micron at $1,048.51 (-0.31%) ahead of earnings.

title: Micron (MU) Earnings Report Q3 2026 — Crushes Estimates
summary: Micron (MU) Earnings Report Q3 2026 — Crushes Estimates
extracted_text: Revenue $41.46 billion vs LSEG consensus $35.84 billion. Adjusted EPS $25.11 vs $20.78 estimate. Net income $28.24 billion ($24.46/share) vs $1.89 billion ($1.68/share) year-ago. Gross margin 84.9%. Q4 guidance revenue ~$50 billion vs analyst forecast $43.58 billion. Data center revenue $11.5 billion (>7x YoY). Cloud memory $13.77 billion (>300% YoY). Mobile & client $11.52 billion (>250% YoY). Automotive & embedded $4.63 billion (>4x YoY). 16 long-term contracts with data center operators and automakers. Shareholders to receive $0.15 per share dividend in July. Stock up ~10% in extended trading. Up ~700% over one year, market cap beyond $1 trillion.

title: S&P 500, Nasdaq Fall for 3rd Straight Day; Oil Prices Drop to Lowest Level Since Start of War
summary: S&P 500, Nasdaq Fall for 3rd Straight Day; Oil Prices Drop to Lowest Level Since Start of War
extracted_text: S&P 500 -0.3%, Nasdaq -0.7%, Dow +0.3%. WTI crude dropped 4.4% to $70/barrel, lowest since Iran war. Down ~40% from wartime high of ~$113. Brent -5% to $73.25. Gold slumped >3% to $4,005/oz, lowest since November. Silver -6% to $58. Bitcoin ~$59,700. 10Y Treasury 4.41% (down from 4.50% yesterday). 2Y yield 4.15% near highest this year. DXY 101.60 (+0.2%). Thursday PCE: Headline 4.1% YoY, Core 3.4% YoY. CME FedWatch pricing at least one rate hike by year-end. JPMorgan target 7,800 (+6%), BCA Research 8,100 (+10%). Cerebras: Q1 revenue $193.4M (+$10M beat), but gross margin guidance 36-38% vs 47% Q1. Stock -19%, now -40% from first-day close. Morgan Stanley's Andrew Slimmon: sell-off tied to shifting rate expectations. Wedbush (Dan Ives): Korea's KOSPI sell-off spilling into U.S. tech.

title: U.S. Treasury Yields Fall, Dollar Firms
summary: U.S. Treasury Yields Fall, Dollar Firms
extracted_text: 10-year Treasury yield fell 1.2 basis points to 4.480% per Tradeweb. DXY dollar index rose 0.2% to 101.63. DHF Capital S.A.'s Bas Kooijman: 'The expected tightening could continue to fuel the dollar's strength and could leave bond yields elevated.' PCE inflation data, the Fed's favored inflation gauge, will take center stage Thursday. Strong print could cement expectations for a September rate hike.

title: Markets Today June 24 2026: Nasdaq Falls, AI Stocks Crash, Micron Earnings and Fed Stress Tests in Focus
summary: Markets Today June 24 2026: Nasdaq Falls, AI Stocks Crash, Micron Earnings and Fed Stress Tests in Focus
extracted_text: Combined 2026 capex of Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta surpassed $452 billion. Alphabet lost John Jumper (Nobel laureate, DeepMind) to Anthropic and Noam Shazeer (Gemini co-lead) to OpenAI. GOOGL fell 6-10% over two sessions. GOOGL Q1 2026 FCF -47% YoY to $10.12B. AMZN -4%, TTM FCF collapsed 95% to $1.2B. Fed rate hike probability by Dec 2026: 87%. DXY 101.65 (2026 high). Goldman Sachs cut gold target to $4,900 from $5,400. USD/JPY near 161 — weakest yen since 1980s. Fed stress test: 32 banks, severe scenario unemployment 10%, home prices -30%, CRE -39%. Capital requirements unchanged until 2027.

title: Oil Plunges to Lowest Since U.S.-Iran Conflict Erupted
summary: Oil Plunges to Lowest Since U.S.-Iran Conflict Erupted
extracted_text: WTI crude settled down 3.9% at $70.34/barrel. Brent fell 4.3% to $73.74/barrel. Market pricing in rapid restoration of Persian Gulf supply as conflict fears ease. Robert Yawger (Mizuho): 'Once storage draws down, oil producers can ramp up production and return to business as usual — as long as the U.S. and Iran reach an agreement in the 60-day negotiation period.' Yawger expects Trump administration to extend negotiations into August, avoiding military flare-up before midterm elections where 'affordability will be a major issue.'

title: Record Margin Debt Raises Leverage Alarms — $1.4 Trillion in May
summary: Record Margin Debt Raises Leverage Alarms — $1.4 Trillion in May
extracted_text: Margin debt surged to $1.4 trillion in May (Finra data), a 54% YoY jump from $920.96 billion and up from $1.3 trillion in April. All-time high for leverage in U.S. equity market. Mark Hackett (Nationwide): 'Investor leverage has reached historic levels across several dimensions, reflecting the lottery mentality of some retail investors. The drivers are connected, including the AI-driven market rally, the explosive growth in levered and single-stock ETFs, and rising interest rates.' Hackett sees parallels to dot-com bubble but notes leverage appears contained to specific market segments. Sentiment surveys not yet signaling irrational exuberance for overall market.

title: Federal Reserve 2026 Bank Stress Test Results Released — Capital Requirements Unchanged Through 2027
summary: Federal Reserve 2026 Bank Stress Test Results Released — Capital Requirements Unchanged Through 2027
extracted_text: 32 major US banks examined (JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, etc.). Severe scenario: unemployment 10%, home prices -30%, commercial real estate -39%. Capital requirements will not change until 2027 while Fed reviews its methodology. Results released at 4:00 p.m. ET on June 24, 2026.

title: PCE Inflation Preview: May Data Could Cement Rate Hike Expectations
summary: PCE Inflation Preview: May Data Could Cement Rate Hike Expectations
extracted_text: Thursday's PCE Inflation Report (May): Headline PCE expected 4.1% YoY (up from 3.8% in April) — highest since 2023. Core PCE expected 3.4% YoY (up from 3.3%) — highest since October 2023. Driven by gasoline prices but core sticky. Core PCE is Fed's preferred gauge. Market already pricing at least one rate hike by year-end (CME FedWatch). 'Rising core inflation could put more pressure on the Fed to use its main inflation-fighting tool: increasing the fed funds rate.'

title: Chip Carnage: SOXX Falls 2.6%, Nasdaq Fumbles After Brief Rally
summary: Chip Carnage: SOXX Falls 2.6%, Nasdaq Fumbles After Brief Rally
extracted_text: iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) fell 2.6%. Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGS) dropped 0.7%. Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF rose 0.8%. Daniel O'Regan (Mizuho): 'Semis have tried to rebound for most of the session, but there's clear bifurcation within the group. Software is also mixed, with continued weakness showing up in the neoCloud names.' Micron at $1,048.51 (-0.31%) ahead of post-close earnings. O'Regan: 'Consensus view is that Micron is now one of the most important stocks in the world (arguably #3 behind NVDA and GOOG), though its immediate post-earnings track record remains mixed at best — the stock has traded down after five of the past six quarters.'

title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 Fall Into Decline with Micron Earnings on Deck
summary: Stocks opened higher but fell by lunchtime. WEN surged >40% on Reddit meme rally (new CEO/CFO, high short interest). HTZ -27%. CBRS -16% on gross margin guidance cut. Oil fell on Iran Strait of Hormuz breakthrough. Treasury Sec Bessent confident of 3% GDP growth. Senate War Powers vote 50-48 for non-binding resolution to end Iran hostilities.
extracted_text: Nasdaq -0.18% midday (reversed from gains), S&P 500 -0.01%, Russell 2000 +0.41%. Micron consensus EPS $20.83, revenue $35.75B (FactSet). Louis Navellier: 'The big news this week is expected to be Micron Technology's earnings announcement... grand finale to a stunning earnings season.' James DePorre: 'The bounce sets up Micron Technology as the most important single event of the week.' WEN surged >40% at peak on Reddit meme rally. HTZ -27.3%. CBRS -16.1%. CASY -6.8%. SLGN -9.5%. SPCX -1.61%. WTI crude -1.6% to $72.06. Brent -1.6% to $75.86. Trump ordered DOJ probe of oil companies for 'gouging.' Bessent: 'We can have something with a three in front of it this year.' Senate vote 50-48. Bitcoin $61,066 (-2.2%). Gold $4,082 (-1.62%). Silver $61.49 (-1.65%).

title: Schwab Market Update: Wall Street Rebounds Awaiting Micron, PCE Prices
summary: Early morning snapshot: stocks opened higher after Tuesday's tech rout. Micron earnings + PCE Thursday are next catalysts. FDX -6% despite beat (guidance disappointed). MU +4% early. Gold -2%, silver -4% on dollar strength. JPMorgan raised year-end S&P 500 target to 7,800. SOX held 21-day MA despite -8% Tuesday. Breadth improving (58% SPX above 50-day MA). Thursday data: PCE headline +4.0% YoY, core +3.4% expected. Q1 GDP final estimate 1.6%. Jobless claims forecast 225K.
extracted_text: Early morning (9:13 AM ET): Stocks opened solidly higher aided by falling crude and Treasury yields. FDX -6% despite beating estimates — FY revenue growth 11%, adjusted EPS $16.90-$18.10 below prior year $20.24. MU +4% early after -13% Tuesday. Gold -2%, silver -4% on dollar strength and rate-hike probabilities. SOX held 21-day moving average despite -8% Tuesday. JPMorgan raised year-end SPX target to 7,800. Breadth: 58% of SPX above 50-day MA. Thursday: PCE headline +4.0% YoY, core +3.4% expected. Q1 GDP final 1.6%. Claims 225K forecast. S&P 500 supports: 50-day MA 7,339 and 61.8% Fibonacci 7,120. ALPHABET (GOOGL) replacing Verizon in DJIA effective next Monday.

title: Current Price of Oil as of June 24, 2026
summary: Brent crude $75.57/bbl at 8:45AM (-$2.47, -3.16% from yesterday). 1-month change -28.04% from $105.03. Trump ordered DOJ probe of oil companies for failing to lower pump prices as crude costs drop. Strategic Petroleum Reserve remains emergency backstop.
extracted_text: Brent crude benchmark $75.57 per barrel at 8:45 a.m. ET. Day-over-day change -$2.47 from $78.04 (-3.16%). 1-month change -28.04% from $105.03. 1-year change +10.83% from $68.18. Trump ordered DOJ to investigate oil companies for 'gouging' on gasoline prices. National average gasoline $3.93/gal, -15% from peak but +30% above pre-war. 'Rockets and feathers' phenomenon: oil spikes quickly push up gas prices, but declines ease gradually.

title: Federal Reserve Issues FOMC Statement — Holds Rates at 3.50-3.75%
summary: [HISTORICAL CONTEXT — June 17 FOMC] Fed held federal funds rate at 3.50-3.75%, unanimous vote. Next meeting July 28-29. Market pricing 87% probability of rate hike by December.
extracted_text: The Committee decided to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate at 3-1/2 to 3-3/4 percent. Vote was unanimous. This is the most recent FOMC decision (June 17, 2026). Market now pricing 87% probability of at least one rate hike by December 2026 (CME FedWatch). Next FOMC meeting: July 28-29, 2026.
